text: What is the primary role of a goalkeeper?
explanation: The goalkeeper's main job is to prevent the opposing team from scoring.

text: Which part of the body can a goalkeeper use inside the penalty area that outfield players cannot?
explanation: Goalkeepers can use their hands within their own penalty area.

text: What color jersey does a goalkeeper typically wear?
choices: A different color from all other players
explanation: Goalkeepers must wear a distinguishing color from teammates, opponents, and referees.

text: Which legendary Italian goalkeeper is known as 'Superman'?
explanation: Gianluigi Buffon earned the nickname 'Superman' for his extraordinary saves.

text: Which goalkeeper has won the most World Cup Golden Glove awards?
explanation: Manuel Neuer won the Golden Glove at the 2014 World Cup.

text: What is the 6-yard box also known as?
explanation: The 6-yard box is officially called the goal area.

text: Which goalkeeper scored the most career goals?
explanation: Rogério Ceni scored over 130 goals in his career, mostly from free kicks and penalties.

text: Who was the first goalkeeper to win the Ballon d'Or?
explanation: Lev Yashin won the Ballon d'Or in 1963, the only goalkeeper ever to do so.

text: What famous save technique involves diving with a scorpion-like kick behind the back?
explanation: René Higuita popularized the scorpion kick save in 1995 against England.

text: Can a goalkeeper handle a deliberate back-pass from a teammate?
choices: No, it results in an indirect free kick
Yes, but only in extra time
Only if the ball crosses the 6-yard box
explanation: The back-pass rule (1992) prohibits goalkeepers from handling deliberate passes from teammates.

text: Which goalkeeper captained Spain to their 2010 World Cup victory?
explanation: Iker Casillas was Spain's captain during their triumphant 2010 World Cup campaign.

text: What is the standard number of defenders in a 4-3-3 formation?
explanation: The 4-3-3 formation uses 4 defenders.

text: What does a 'clean sheet' mean?
choices: A match where no fouls were committed
A match where the team did not concede a goal
A match with no yellow cards
explanation: A clean sheet means the team conceded zero goals in the match.

text: Which defender position plays in the center of the back line?
explanation: Center backs play in the middle of the defensive line.

text: What is an offside trap?
choices: A defensive strategy where the line pushes up to catch attackers offside
explanation: The offside trap involves defenders stepping forward in unison before a pass to catch attackers offside.

text: Which Spanish defender holds the record for most international caps for Spain?
explanation: Sergio Ramos holds Spain's record with 180 international caps.

text: What is a 'sweeper' (libero) in football?
choices: A defender who plays behind the main defensive line
explanation: The sweeper plays behind the defensive line and 'sweeps up' any balls that get past the main defenders.

text: Which Italian defensive system is famous for its tight man-marking?
explanation: Catenaccio is an Italian defensive tactic emphasizing tight man-marking and a sweeper.

text: Which defender won the 2006 Ballon d'Or?
explanation: Fabio Cannavaro won the 2006 Ballon d'Or after captaining Italy to the World Cup title.

text: Which defender has scored the most goals in Champions League history?
explanation: Sergio Ramos holds the record for most goals by a defender in Champions League history.

text: Who is the youngest player to captain a Champions League winning team?
explanation: Paolo Maldini captained AC Milan in Champions League victories at a remarkably young age.

text: What is a 'tackle' in football?
choices: An attempt to take the ball from an opponent
explanation: A tackle is the act of challenging an opponent to win possession of the ball.

text: Which country is Paolo Maldini from?
explanation: Paolo Maldini is Italian and spent his entire career at AC Milan.

text: Which defender has made the most appearances in World Cup history?
explanation: Lothar Matthäus holds the record for most World Cup appearances with 25 matches.

text: In what situation is a penalty kick awarded?
choices: Foul inside the penalty area by the defending team
explanation: A penalty kick is awarded when the defending team commits a foul inside their own penalty area.

text: What is a 'last-man' foul?
choices: A foul by the last defender that denies a clear goal-scoring opportunity
explanation: A last-man foul denies an obvious goal-scoring opportunity and typically results in a red card.

text: Which left-back is famous for his powerful free kicks and played for Real Madrid and Brazil?
explanation: Roberto Carlos was known for his incredible left-foot free kicks and played for Real Madrid from 1996 to 2007.

text: How many defenders typically form a back three?
explanation: A back three uses 3 center-backs, often with wing-backs providing width.

text: What does 'marking' mean in defensive play?
choices: Staying close to an opponent to prevent them receiving the ball
explanation: Marking means closely guarding an opponent to prevent them from receiving or playing the ball effectively.

text: Which defender played 647 consecutive league matches for AC Milan without missing a game?
explanation: Franco Baresi was renowned for his consistency and longevity at AC Milan.

text: Which nation did Franz Beckenbauer represent?
explanation: Franz Beckenbauer represented West Germany and is considered one of the greatest defenders ever.

text: What is the main role of a central midfielder?
choices: Link defense and attack, control the tempo
explanation: Central midfielders connect defense to attack and dictate the pace of the game.

text: What does 'possession' mean in football?
choices: How many goals a team scores
The percentage of time a team has the ball
explanation: Possession refers to the percentage of time a team controls the ball during a match.

text: Which country invented 'tiki-taka' style of play?
explanation: Spain and FC Barcelona are credited with developing the tiki-taka passing style.

text: Which midfielder is known as 'The Architect' and played for Barcelona and Spain?
explanation: Xavi Hernández was nicknamed 'The Architect' for his ability to orchestrate play.

text: What is a 'box-to-box' midfielder?
choices: A midfielder who covers the full length of the pitch, defending and attacking
explanation: A box-to-box midfielder contributes at both ends of the pitch, covering penalty area to penalty area.

text: Which French midfielder won 3 consecutive Ballon d'Or awards in the 1980s?
explanation: Michel Platini won the Ballon d'Or in 1983, 1984, and 1985.

text: Who scored the famous volley goal in the 2002 Champions League final?
explanation: Zinedine Zidane scored a stunning left-foot volley in the 2002 Champions League final against Bayer Leverkusen.

text: Which midfielder has the most assists in Champions League history?
explanation: Lionel Messi holds the record for most assists in Champions League history.

text: What is a 'through ball'?
choices: A pass played into space behind the defense for a teammate to run onto
explanation: A through ball splits the defense and allows an attacker to run onto it in a goal-scoring position.

text: Which German midfielder captained Bayern Munich and Germany, known for his versatility?
explanation: Bastian Schweinsteiger captained both Bayern Munich and Germany and could play multiple positions.

text: Who holds the record for most La Liga assists of all time?
explanation: Lionel Messi holds the record for the most assists in La Liga history.

text: What is a 'holding midfielder' (number 6)?
choices: A defensive midfielder who shields the back line
explanation: The holding midfielder sits in front of the defense, breaking up opponent attacks and distributing the ball.

text: Which midfielder scored in two different World Cup finals?
explanation: Zinedine Zidane scored in the 1998 World Cup final (2 goals) and the 2006 final (1 goal via penalty).

text: What does 'dribbling' mean in football?
choices: Passing the ball to a teammate
Running with the ball at your feet while maintaining control
explanation: Dribbling is the skill of moving the ball forward while keeping it under close control.

text: Which midfielder scored the winning goal in the 2010 World Cup final?
explanation: Andrés Iniesta scored the winning goal in extra time against Netherlands in the 2010 World Cup final.

text: What is a 'hat-trick'?
explanation: A hat-trick is when a player scores three goals in a single match.

text: Which player has scored the most goals in FIFA World Cup history?
explanation: Miroslav Klose scored 16 goals across four World Cups for Germany.

text: What is the 'Golden Boot' award?
choices: Award for the top scorer in a tournament
explanation: The Golden Boot is awarded to the top scorer in a football tournament.

text: Who won the most Ballon d'Or awards?
explanation: Lionel Messi has won the Ballon d'Or 8 times, more than any other player.

text: Which striker scored 91 goals in a calendar year (2012)?
explanation: Lionel Messi scored 91 goals in 2012, breaking Gerd Müller's record of 85 goals in 1972.

text: What is the 'False 9' position?
choices: A winger who plays as a center back
A center forward who drops deep into midfield to create space
A defender who acts as a striker
explanation: The False 9 drops deep from the striker position to link play and create space for teammates.

text: Who scored the 'Hand of God' goal?
explanation: Diego Maradona scored the 'Hand of God' goal against England in the 1986 World Cup quarter-final.

text: Which Brazilian striker won the World Cup twice (1994 and 2002)?
explanation: Ronaldo Nazário won the World Cup in 1994 (as a squad member) and 2002 (as the star striker).

text: What is a 'volley' in football?
choices: Kicking the ball while it is in the air before it bounces
explanation: A volley is striking the ball while it is still in the air.

text: Which club did Erling Haaland join from Borussia Dortmund?
explanation: Erling Haaland transferred from Borussia Dortmund to Manchester City in 2022.

text: Who is the all-time top scorer in Champions League history?
explanation: Cristiano Ronaldo holds the record for the most goals in Champions League history.

text: Which country did the legendary striker Pelé represent?
explanation: Pelé played for Brazil and won three FIFA World Cups (1958, 1962, 1970).

text: Which striker scored the fastest hat-trick in Premier League history?
explanation: Sadio Mané scored the fastest Premier League hat-trick in 2 minutes 56 seconds for Southampton against Aston Villa in 2015.

text: What does 'offside' mean for an attacker?
choices: Being behind the ball when it is passed
Being nearer to the opponent's goal line than the second-last defender when the ball is played
explanation: An attacker is offside if they are nearer to the goal line than both the ball and the second-last defender.

text: Which World Cup saw the most goals scored in a single tournament by one player (13 goals)?
explanation: Just Fontaine scored 13 goals at the 1958 World Cup, a record that still stands.

text: Which defender has won the most Champions League titles?
explanation: Marcelo won 5 Champions League titles with Real Madrid.

text: Which midfielder has scored the most goals in World Cup finals history?
explanation: Pelé scored in multiple World Cup finals, accumulating the most final goals.

text: What is a 'header clearance'?
choices: Using the head to clear the ball away from danger in the defensive area
explanation: A header clearance is when a defender uses their head to direct the ball away from their goal.

text: Which French legend presented the 2022 Ballon d'Or to Karim Benzema?
explanation: Zinedine Zidane presented the 2022 Ballon d'Or to Karim Benzema.

text: Who scored the fastest goal in FIFA World Cup history?
explanation: Hakan Şükür scored after 11 seconds for Turkey against South Korea in 2002.

text: Who scored the winning goal in the 2010 FIFA World Cup final?
explanation: Andrés Iniesta scored in extra time against the Netherlands.

text: Who is Real Madrid's all-time top scorer?
explanation: Cristiano Ronaldo is Real Madrid's all-time leading scorer.

text: Which Italian defender captained Italy to the 2006 World Cup and won the Ballon d'Or that same year?
explanation: Fabio Cannavaro captained Italy in 2006 and won the Ballon d'Or.

text: Who assisted Andrés Iniesta's winning goal in the 2010 World Cup final?
explanation: Cesc Fàbregas provided the assist for Iniesta's goal.

text: Which goalkeeper won the Golden Ball at the 2002 FIFA World Cup?
explanation: Oliver Kahn won the Golden Ball in 2002 after outstanding performances for Germany.

text: Which Brazilian striker was nicknamed 'O Fenômeno'?
explanation: Ronaldo Nazário was famously known as 'O Fenômeno'.

text: Which defender scored Real Madrid's dramatic equalizer in the 2014 Champions League final against Atlético Madrid?
explanation: Sergio Ramos scored the famous 93rd-minute equalizer in Lisbon.

text: Which player won the 2007 Ballon d'Or?
explanation: Kaká won the Ballon d'Or in 2007 after starring for AC Milan.

text: Which country did Hakan Şükür represent internationally?
explanation: Hakan Şükür was one of Turkey's most famous strikers.

text: Which goalkeeper became famous for the 'scorpion kick' save?
explanation: René Higuita made the scorpion kick save famous in 1995.

text: Which Croatian midfielder won the Ballon d'Or in 2018?
explanation: Luka Modrić ended the Messi-Ronaldo streak by winning the 2018 Ballon d'Or.

text: Who scored the winning goal in the 2014 FIFA World Cup final?
explanation: Mario Götze scored in extra time against Argentina.

text: Which legendary AC Milan defender was known as 'Il Capitano'?
explanation: Paolo Maldini is one of the greatest defenders in football history.

text: Who is the only goalkeeper to have won the Ballon d'Or?
explanation: Lev Yashin won the Ballon d'Or in 1963.

text: Who scored the famous bicycle kick goal for Real Madrid against Juventus in the Champions League in 2018?
explanation: Cristiano Ronaldo scored one of the most famous bicycle kicks in Champions League history.

text: Which Spanish midfielder was known for controlling games with short passing and vision, and played for Barcelona?
explanation: Xavi was one of the masters of possession football with Barcelona and Spain.

text: Which national team did Carles Puyol represent?
explanation: Carles Puyol was a key defender for Spain and won the 2010 World Cup.

text: Which player scored 13 goals in a single FIFA World Cup tournament, a record that still stands?
explanation: Just Fontaine scored 13 goals at the 1958 World Cup.

text: How wide is a regulation football goal?
explanation: A standard football goal is 7.32 metres (8 yards) wide and 2.44 metres tall.

text: Which goalkeeper won the Premier League Golden Glove a record 5 times?
explanation: Pepe Reina won the Premier League Golden Glove award five times with Liverpool.

text: Which goalkeeper conceded the fewest goals in a single Premier League season (38 games)?
explanation: Petr Čech conceded only 15 goals in the 2004–05 Premier League season for Chelsea.

text: What is the maximum number of steps a goalkeeper can take while holding the ball?
explanation: A goalkeeper can take a maximum of 4 steps while holding the ball before they must release it.

text: Which goalkeeper famously saved three penalties in a 2005 Champions League final shootout?
explanation: Jerzy Dudek made three penalty saves for Liverpool against AC Milan in Istanbul 2005.

text: Which national team did legendary goalkeeper Lev Yashin play for?
explanation: Lev Yashin, known as the 'Black Spider', was the Soviet Union's legendary goalkeeper.

text: Which goalkeeper holds the record for the most clean sheets in Premier League history?
explanation: Petr Čech holds the Premier League record with 202 clean sheets.

text: From where is a goal kick taken?
explanation: A goal kick can be taken from anywhere inside the goal area (the 6-yard box).

text: Which goalkeeper scored a competitive goal directly from a drop kick?
explanation: Alisson Becker scored a dramatic header for Liverpool against West Brom in May 2021.

text: Which World Cup saw Germany goalkeeper Oliver Kahn win the Golden Ball award despite finishing as runners-up?
explanation: Oliver Kahn won the Golden Ball at the 2002 World Cup despite Germany losing the final to Brazil.

text: Which goalkeeper kept a clean sheet in a World Cup final and never conceded in 1,142 minutes of international football?
explanation: Dino Zoff went 1,142 minutes without conceding for Italy and captained them to the 1982 World Cup.

text: What colour card results in a player being immediately sent off?
explanation: A red card means the player is immediately sent off and cannot be replaced.

text: Which Brazilian right-back won the Champions League with both Barcelona and Juventus?
explanation: Dani Alves won the Champions League with Barcelona and later won Serie A and Coppa Italia with Juventus.

text: Who is the only defender to win the FIFA World Player of the Year award?
explanation: Fabio Cannavaro won the FIFA World Player of the Year in 2006 after Italy's World Cup triumph.

text: What restart takes place after the ball goes out of play over the touchline?
explanation: A throw-in is awarded when the ball crosses the touchline.

text: Which English defender captained Chelsea to back-to-back Premier League titles in 2005 and 2006?
explanation: John Terry captained Chelsea through their historic back-to-back league titles under José Mourinho.

text: Which defender was known as 'The Kaiser' and won the World Cup as both player and manager?
explanation: Franz Beckenbauer won the World Cup in 1974 as a player and in 1990 as Germany's manager.

text: Which nation does Virgil van Dijk represent internationally?
explanation: Virgil van Dijk captains the Netherlands national team.

text: What is a 'zonal marking' system?
choices: Each defender man-marks a specific opponent
Defenders guard zones of the pitch rather than specific players
explanation: In zonal marking, defenders cover assigned areas of the pitch instead of following individual opponents.

text: Which English club went an entire 49-game unbeaten league run between 2003 and 2004?
explanation: Arsenal's 'Invincibles' went 49 Premier League games unbeaten under Arsène Wenger.

text: Which Portuguese defender plays for Manchester City and is known for his leadership at the back?
explanation: Rúben Dias won the Premier League with Manchester City and was named the league's best player in 2020–21.

text: Which Dutch defender captained his country to the 1988 European Championship?
explanation: Ruud Gullit captained the Netherlands to their only major international trophy at Euro 1988.

text: How many players are on each side in a standard football match?
explanation: Each team fields 11 players, including the goalkeeper, in a standard match.

text: Which Spanish defender scored the winning goal for Barcelona in the 2009 Champions League final?
explanation: Gerard Piqué scored Barcelona's second goal in the 2009 Champions League final win over Manchester United.

text: Which left-back won 5 Champions League titles and 9 La Liga titles, all with Real Madrid?
explanation: Marcelo won 5 Champions League titles with Real Madrid, making him the most decorated player in the competition.

text: What trophy is awarded to the best player at each FIFA World Cup?
explanation: The Golden Ball is awarded to the best player of the FIFA World Cup tournament.

text: Which Brazilian midfielder is famous for his dribbling, tricks, and playing for FC Barcelona?
explanation: Ronaldinho dazzled the world with his tricks and flair during his time at FC Barcelona.

text: Which midfielder won the Champions League with three different clubs?
explanation: Clarence Seedorf won the Champions League with Ajax (1995), Real Madrid (1998), and AC Milan (2003, 2007).

text: What does 'pressing' mean in modern football?
choices: Aggressively chasing the ball high up the pitch to win it back quickly
Playing a long ball over the defense
explanation: Pressing means pressuring the opponent immediately after losing the ball to win it back quickly.

text: Which German midfielder was nicknamed 'Der Bomber der Nation' for his prolific scoring?
explanation: Gerd Müller was nicknamed 'Der Bomber' for his incredible goal-scoring instinct for Bayern Munich and Germany.

text: Which club did Kevin De Bruyne join before moving to Manchester City?
explanation: Kevin De Bruyne starred for VfL Wolfsburg, winning the Bundesliga Player of the Season, before joining Man City.

text: Which midfielder is known as 'La Pulga' (The Flea) due to his small stature?
explanation: Lionel Messi is nicknamed 'La Pulga' because of his small frame and quick, elusive movement.

text: What is a 'free kick' in football?
choices: A kick awarded to the opposing team after a foul
A kick taken without any defenders nearby
A kick awarded to restart the game after a goal
explanation: A free kick is awarded to a team after the opponents commit a foul.

text: Which midfielder scored the winning goal in extra time of the 1998 World Cup final?
explanation: Emmanuel Petit scored the third goal in France's 3–0 win over Brazil in the 1998 World Cup final.

text: Which Italian playmaker was famous for his vision and passing at Juventus in the 1980s?
explanation: Michel Platini was the dominant force in Italian football at Juventus, winning three Serie A titles.

text: Who scored the 'Ghost Goal' for England against Germany in the 2010 World Cup?
explanation: Frank Lampard's shot clearly crossed the line against Germany but was not awarded in the 2010 World Cup.

text: What is a 'corner kick'?
choices: A kick awarded when the ball goes out over the byline last touched by the attacking team
A kick awarded when the ball goes out over the byline last touched by the defending team
A restart after the ball goes out of play over the touchline
A free kick from the edge of the box
explanation: A corner kick is awarded to the attacking team when the defending team last touches the ball over their own byline.

text: Which midfielder was named the best player at the 2018 FIFA World Cup?
explanation: Luka Modrić won the Golden Ball at the 2018 World Cup after inspiring Croatia to the final.

text: Which Spanish midfielder is the all-time leading assist provider in Champions League history at his retirement?
explanation: Xavi Hernández made a record 21 assists in the Champions League during his career with Barcelona.

text: Which club's stadium is known as 'The Theatre of Dreams'?
explanation: Old Trafford, home of Manchester United, is nicknamed 'The Theatre of Dreams'.

text: What is the penalty spot distance from the goal line?
explanation: The penalty spot is exactly 11 metres (12 yards) from the goal line.

text: Which striker scored a hat-trick in the 2022 World Cup final but still ended up on the losing side?
explanation: Kylian Mbappé scored a hat-trick in the 2022 World Cup final but France lost to Argentina on penalties.

text: Which Dutch striker scored 35 goals in a single Bundesliga season in 2022?
explanation: Erling Haaland scored 35 Bundesliga goals in the 2021–22 season for Borussia Dortmund.

text: What is an 'assist' in football?
choices: A pass or action that directly leads to a goal being scored
A shot that hits the post
explanation: An assist is credited to the player whose pass or action directly leads to a goal.

text: Which French striker was the top scorer at Euro 2000?
explanation: Patrick Kluivert of the Netherlands finished as joint top scorer at Euro 2000 with 5 goals.

text: Who scored a famous backheel goal for Juventus against Internazionale in the 2015 Coppa Italia semi-final?
explanation: Carlos Tévez scored an outrageous backheel against Inter Milan in the 2015 Coppa Italia.

text: Which country does Karim Benzema represent internationally?
explanation: Karim Benzema plays for France, despite having Algerian heritage.

text: Which striker holds the record for most goals in a single Premier League season with 34 goals?
explanation: Andrew Cole scored 34 Premier League goals for Newcastle United in the 1993–94 season.

text: Which Argentine striker was sold by Barcelona for a world record fee to PSG in 2017?
explanation: Neymar Jr. transferred from Barcelona to PSG in 2017 for a world record €222 million fee.

text: Which Italian striker wore the number 9 shirt for Italy and won the 2006 World Cup?
explanation: Luca Toni wore the number 9 shirt and was Italy's main striker at the 2006 World Cup.

text: Which English striker is the all-time Premier League top scorer?
explanation: Alan Shearer scored 260 Premier League goals, a record that still stands.

text: Who scored the goal that eliminated France from Euro 2004 with a last-minute strike?
explanation: Angelos Charisteas scored the only goal as Greece shocked France in the Euro 2004 quarter-final.

text: What nationality is striker Robert Lewandowski?
explanation: Robert Lewandowski is Polish and has been one of Europe's top scorers for over a decade.

text: Which striker scored in 11 consecutive Champions League matches, a record?
explanation: Ruud van Nistelrooy scored in 11 consecutive Champions League games for Manchester United in 2002–03.

text: Which Argentine goalkeeper won the 2022 FIFA World Cup with his country?
explanation: Emiliano Martínez was crucial in Argentina's 2022 World Cup triumph, making key saves in the semi-final and final shootouts.

text: What does the acronym 'VAR' stand for?
explanation: VAR stands for Video Assistant Referee, a system introduced to assist match officials.

text: How long is a standard football match (excluding extra time)?
explanation: A standard football match lasts 90 minutes, divided into two 45-minute halves.

text: Which country won the first ever FIFA World Cup in 1930?
explanation: Uruguay hosted and won the first FIFA World Cup in 1930, defeating Argentina 4–2 in the final.

text: Which goalkeeper went unbeaten for 13 consecutive World Cup matches, a record?
explanation: Peter Shilton went a record 13 World Cup matches without conceding a goal for England.

text: Which defender has won the most Champions League titles as a player?
explanation: Marcelo won 5 Champions League titles with Real Madrid between 2013 and 2022.

text: Inside which area is a goalkeeper allowed to handle the ball?
explanation: A goalkeeper is only allowed to use their hands inside their own penalty area.

text: Which Italian goalkeeper won the 2006 FIFA World Cup with Italy?
explanation: Gianluigi Buffon was Italy's starting goalkeeper in their 2006 World Cup-winning campaign.

text: Which Brazilian goalkeeper captained Brazil in the 1970 World Cup final?
explanation: Félix was Brazil's goalkeeper and captain in the 1970 World Cup final against Italy.

text: What is the crossbar?
choices: The line in the middle of the pitch
The horizontal bar joining the two goalposts
The line of the penalty area
explanation: The crossbar is the horizontal bar at the top of the goal.

text: Which goalkeeper was England's number one at the 1966 World Cup and later made the famous 'Banks of England' save?
explanation: Gordon Banks is one of England's greatest goalkeepers and is remembered for his save from Pelé in 1970.

text: Which goalkeeper became the first ever winner of the Yashin Trophy in 2019?
explanation: Alisson Becker won the first Yashin Trophy, the award created for the world's best goalkeeper.

text: What is a goalkeeper's main duty during a penalty kick?
choices: Stop the ball from entering the goal
explanation: The goalkeeper's job during a penalty is to try to save the shot.

text: Which goalkeeper started for France in the 2018 World Cup final?
explanation: Hugo Lloris captained France and started in their 2018 World Cup final victory.

text: Which goalkeeper scored more than 100 career goals, mostly from penalties and free kicks?
explanation: Rogério Ceni is the highest-scoring goalkeeper in football history.

text: Which Spanish goalkeeper was Barcelona's regular starter during much of Pep Guardiola's era?
explanation: Víctor Valdés was Barcelona's first-choice goalkeeper during their dominant Guardiola years.

text: What is a rebound save situation?
choices: When the ball comes back into play after a save
When the goalkeeper takes a throw-in
When the ball hits the corner flag
explanation: A rebound happens when the goalkeeper saves the ball but it remains in play.

text: Which goalkeeper won the Golden Glove at the 2014 FIFA World Cup?
explanation: Manuel Neuer won the Golden Glove after an outstanding tournament for Germany in 2014.

text: What is the main job of a center-back?
choices: Protect the area in front of goal and stop attackers
explanation: Center-backs are primarily responsible for defending central areas and stopping attacks.

text: Which defender captained Barcelona in the 2011 Champions League final?
explanation: Carles Puyol was Barcelona's captain during the 2011 Champions League final.

text: Which German defender scored in the 2014 World Cup final after coming on as a substitute?
explanation: Mario Götze scored the extra-time winner in the 2014 World Cup final.

text: What does a defender do when they 'block' a shot?
choices: Stop the shot from reaching goal
Kick it for a throw-in automatically
explanation: Blocking a shot means getting in the way to prevent the ball from reaching the goal.

text: Which legendary Italian defender wore number 3 for AC Milan for most of his career?
explanation: Paolo Maldini famously wore the number 3 shirt for AC Milan.

text: Which defender won Euro 2000 and the 1998 World Cup with France and later coached France to the 2018 World Cup title?
explanation: Didier Deschamps won major trophies as a player and later coached France to the 2018 World Cup.

text: What is a wing-back?
choices: A wide defender with both defensive and attacking duties
A striker who drifts to the wing
A center-back who never leaves the box
explanation: A wing-back plays on the flank and contributes both defensively and offensively.

text: Which Spanish defender scored the winning goal in the 2010 World Cup semi-final against Germany?
explanation: Carles Puyol scored with a header to send Spain into the 2010 World Cup final.

text: Which defender is known as 'Der Kaiser'?
explanation: Franz Beckenbauer was nicknamed 'Der Kaiser' and is one of the greatest defenders ever.

text: What is a headed clearance?
choices: A goal scored with the head
A defender using their head to remove danger
explanation: A headed clearance is when a defender uses their head to clear the ball away from danger.

text: Which Brazilian defender is famous for his powerful free kicks and long-range shots from left-back?
explanation: Roberto Carlos became famous for his incredible left foot and spectacular free kicks.

text: Which Dutch defender scored twice for the Netherlands in the Euro 1988 final?
explanation: Ruud Gullit scored in the Euro 1988 final as the Netherlands beat the Soviet Union.

text: What does it mean when defenders 'hold the line'?
choices: They all stay deep inside the box
They keep their shape together across the pitch
explanation: Holding the line means defenders stay coordinated and maintain the defensive shape together.

text: Which Italian defender was captain of Juventus and part of Italy's 2006 World Cup-winning squad?
explanation: Giorgio Chiellini became one of Italy and Juventus's great defensive leaders.

text: What is the job of a defensive midfielder?
choices: Protect the defense and recover the ball
explanation: A defensive midfielder shields the back line and helps win the ball back.

text: Which Spanish midfielder scored the winning goal in the 2010 FIFA World Cup final?
explanation: Andrés Iniesta scored in extra time to give Spain their first World Cup title.

text: Which Dutch midfielder scored one of the most famous volleys in history in the Euro 1988 final?
explanation: Marco van Basten scored an iconic volley in the Euro 1988 final against the Soviet Union.

text: What is a long ball in football?
choices: A high or driven pass played over a long distance
explanation: A long ball is a pass played over a significant distance, often to switch play or find a forward quickly.

text: Which midfielder was nicknamed 'Don Andrés'?
explanation: Andrés Iniesta was affectionately nicknamed 'Don Andrés' because of his class and elegance.

text: Which midfielder won the Ballon d'Or in 2007 after starring for AC Milan?
explanation: Kaká won the 2007 Ballon d'Or after a brilliant year for AC Milan.

text: What is a pass completion rate?
choices: The number of goals from passes
The percentage of passes that successfully reach a teammate
The number of long balls attempted
explanation: Pass completion rate measures how many of a player's passes are accurate.

text: Which Croatian midfielder won the Ballon d'Or in 2018?
explanation: Luka Modrić won the 2018 Ballon d'Or after a brilliant year for club and country.

text: Which midfielder won the Champions League with Ajax, Real Madrid, and AC Milan?
explanation: Clarence Seedorf is famous for winning the Champions League with three different clubs.

text: What does a midfielder do when they 'dictate the tempo'?
choices: Control the speed and rhythm of the game
explanation: Dictating the tempo means controlling how quickly or slowly the team plays.

text: Which French midfielder won three consecutive Ballon d'Or awards in the 1980s?
explanation: Michel Platini won the Ballon d'Or in 1983, 1984, and 1985.

text: Which midfielder scored Germany's winning goal in the 2014 World Cup final assist move before the finish?
explanation: André Schürrle provided the cross for Mario Götze's winning goal in the final.

text: What is an interception in midfield?
choices: Stopping an opponent's pass before it reaches its target
explanation: An interception is when a player reads the pass and cuts it out.

text: Which English midfielder was known for wearing number 8 at Chelsea and scoring many goals from midfield?
explanation: Frank Lampard became one of the highest-scoring midfielders in Premier League history.

text: What is a striker's main job?
choices: Score goals and lead the attack
explanation: A striker's primary responsibility is to score goals and create danger in attack.

text: Which Portuguese forward is Real Madrid's all-time top scorer?
explanation: Cristiano Ronaldo is Real Madrid's all-time leading scorer.

text: Which striker scored twice for Brazil in the 2002 World Cup final?
explanation: Ronaldo Nazário scored both goals in Brazil's 2-0 win over Germany in the 2002 final.

text: What is a header in football?
choices: Kicking the ball in the air
Using the head to play the ball
explanation: A header is when a player uses their head to direct the ball.

text: Which French striker scored the golden goal in the Euro 2000 final?
explanation: David Trezeguet scored the golden goal that won Euro 2000 for France.

text: Which striker scored four goals for Real Madrid in the 2017 Champions League final era campaign final season run and is the all-time top scorer in the competition?
explanation: Cristiano Ronaldo is the all-time top scorer in Champions League history and was central to Real Madrid's 2017 triumph.

text: What is a bicycle kick?
choices: A shot taken with the knee
explanation: A bicycle kick is an overhead acrobatic attempt at goal.

text: Which Norwegian striker joined Manchester City in 2022?
explanation: Erling Haaland joined Manchester City in 2022.

text: Which player scored the 'Hand of God' goal in the 1986 World Cup?
explanation: Diego Maradona scored the famous 'Hand of God' goal against England.

text: What is a near-post finish?
choices: A shot aimed toward the post closest to the attacker
A shot from the halfway line
A volley from outside the box
explanation: A near-post finish is aimed at the side of the goal nearest to the shooter.

text: Which striker is Argentina's all-time top scorer?
explanation: Lionel Messi is Argentina's all-time top scorer.

text: Which striker scored the fastest hat-trick in Premier League history?
explanation: Sadio Mané scored the fastest Premier League hat-trick in 2 minutes 56 seconds.

text: What does a forward do when making a 'run in behind'?
choices: Sprint into space behind the defense
Stay wide near the corner flag only
Move back to their own penalty area
explanation: A run in behind is made to attack the space behind the defensive line.

text: Which Uruguayan striker is nicknamed 'El Pistolero'?
explanation: Luis Suárez is famously known as 'El Pistolero'.

text: Which French striker won the 1991 Ballon d'Or while playing for Marseille?
explanation: Jean-Pierre Papin won the 1991 Ballon d'Or after his brilliant performances for Marseille.

text: What is a first-time finish?
choices: A shot taken without controlling the ball first
The first shot of the game
A penalty taken in the first half
A shot from the center circle
explanation: A first-time finish is struck immediately without taking a touch to control the ball.

text: Which striker scored the winning goal for Spain in the Euro 2008 final?
explanation: Fernando Torres scored the only goal in Spain's Euro 2008 final win over Germany.

text: Which striker scored in both the 1958 and 1970 World Cup finals for Brazil?
explanation: Pelé scored in the 1958 and 1970 World Cup finals for Brazil.

text: Which player won the Golden Boot at the 2014 FIFA World Cup?
explanation: James Rodríguez finished as the top scorer of the 2014 World Cup with 6 goals.

text: Which forward scored the winning goal for Manchester United in the 1999 Champions League final?
explanation: Ole Gunnar Solskjær scored the dramatic stoppage-time winner against Bayern Munich.
